localSourceOfTruth: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5B. Staff identity, organisation boundaries, and acting context
blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5E. Alternative offers, patient choice, and network-facing UX
blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5H. Patient communications, network reminders, manage flows, and practice visibility
blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5I. Hardening, policy assurance, pilot rollout, and formal exit gate
summary: Accessed on 2026-04-23. External sources were used only to sharpen browser-proof technique, accessibility interpretation, status wording discipline, and dense evidence-board hierarchy. The local Phase 0 and Phase 5 contracts remained authoritative wherever product semantics could drift.

sources.title: Accessibility testing | Playwright
borrowedInto: 340 accessibility assertions treat automated scans as support only and keep manual semantic assertions in the browser proofs.
The suite captures high-value accessibility assertions directly in the existing Playwright flows instead of splitting them into a detached audit pass.
rejectedOrConstrained: Axe-style automation was not treated as sufficient evidence for truthful content or focus continuity.

sources.title: Snapshot testing | Playwright
borrowedInto: ARIA snapshots are limited to high-risk regions: appointment family, hub acting-context posture, and mobile artifact/timeline states.
rejectedOrConstrained: Snapshot coverage was not widened into blanket snapshotting because that would obscure the high-risk semantics.

sources.title: Isolation | Playwright
borrowedInto: Each browser project runs in a clean context so selected-anchor persistence and acting-context drift are proved deliberately instead of leaking prior browser state.
rejectedOrConstrained: Isolation guidance did not alter the local continuity law for explicit refresh and restore behavior.

sources.title: Emulation | Playwright
borrowedInto: The 340 project matrix includes mobile, tablet, reduced-motion, and high-zoom proxies as first-class release conditions.
rejectedOrConstrained: Device emulation was constrained to deterministic fixtures instead of pretending to certify real device fleets.

sources.title: Trace viewer | Playwright
borrowedInto: Every 340 browser project emits trace artifacts so the Phase 5 exit gate can inspect interaction history without rerunning the suite.
rejectedOrConstrained: Trace artifacts remain evidence only; they do not replace explicit machine-readable summary rows.

sources.title: Best Practices | Playwright
borrowedInto: The suite uses deterministic fixtures, stable selectors, and role-driven assertions for the highest-risk flows.
rejectedOrConstrained: Best-practice guidance did not justify weakening explicit truth assertions in favor of generic user-journey smoke tests.

sources.title: Understanding Success Criterion 1.4.10: Reflow | WAI | W3C
borrowedInto: The 320px proxy and narrow-screen proofs explicitly verify no horizontal overflow and preserved action reachability.
rejectedOrConstrained: WCAG reflow guidance informed proof shape only; local shell semantics still come from the blueprint.

sources.title: Understanding Success Criterion 4.1.3: Status Messages | WAI | W3C
borrowedInto: Patient and hub status proofs require explicit status semantics instead of color-only state changes.
rejectedOrConstrained: Status-message guidance did not override the local truth vocabulary for pending, provisional, warned, or denied states.

sources.title: Understanding Success Criterion 2.4.13: Focus Appearance | WAI | W3C
borrowedInto: Keyboard-only and modal-focus proofs require visible focus and predictable focus return.
rejectedOrConstrained: The criterion did not alter the blueprint's same-shell continuity rules.

sources.title: Understanding Success Criterion 2.5.8: Target Size (Minimum) | WAI | W3C
borrowedInto: Mobile and folded-shell checks keep the sticky actions and support triggers reachable at narrow breakpoints.
rejectedOrConstrained: Target-size guidance did not change the local dominant-action ordering.

sources.title: Understanding Success Criterion 2.3.3: Animation from Interactions | WAI | W3C
borrowedInto: Reduced-motion is treated as release proof, and the hub shell now proves meaning without motion-heavy transitions.
rejectedOrConstrained: Motion guidance was not used to remove necessary status transitions, only non-essential animation.

sources.title: Dialog (Modal) Pattern | APG | WAI | W3C
borrowedInto: The break-glass modal proof now requires an explicit focus trap and focus return to the invoking chip.
rejectedOrConstrained: The acting-context drawer was intentionally kept as a non-modal drawer; it does not masquerade as a dialog.

sources.title: Disclosure (Show/Hide) Pattern | APG | WAI | W3C
borrowedInto: Timeline and artifact panels preserve explicit `aria-expanded` state and durable labels.
rejectedOrConstrained: Disclosure guidance did not collapse the domain meaning of callback, warning, or provenance panels.

sources.title: Listbox Pattern | APG | WAI | W3C
borrowedInto: Patient choice interaction checks keep keyboard navigation deliberate and avoid mouse-only selection binding.
rejectedOrConstrained: Listbox guidance informed keyboard behavior only; the offer surface remained a choice card grammar rather than a generic listbox clone.

sources.title: Tabs Pattern | APG | WAI | W3C
borrowedInto: The evidence board uses keyboardable tab-like family selection rather than inert static cards.
rejectedOrConstrained: Tabs guidance was constrained to the evidence board controls and was not forced onto product surfaces that are not tabs.

sources.title: Confirmation page – NHS digital service manual
borrowedInto: Patient-facing confirmation proof keeps the main state calm, concise, and honest.
rejectedOrConstrained: Confirmation-page guidance did not allow overclaiming booked or guaranteed outcomes when the truth is still pending.

sources.title: Interruption page – NHS digital service manual
borrowedInto: Denied and stale-scope postures stay explicit and actionable instead of silently redirecting.
rejectedOrConstrained: Interruption guidance was not used to introduce unnecessary blocking screens where same-shell bounded recovery is the local law.

sources.title: Triage – Linear Docs
borrowedInto: The evidence board groups risk families into fast-scanning lanes rather than a raw chronological dump.
rejectedOrConstrained: Linear workflow conventions were not imported as product semantics.

sources.title: Vercel Design
borrowedInto: The evidence board uses a restrained information hierarchy and quiet framing for the release-proof surface.
rejectedOrConstrained: Vercel styling did not override the NHS-adjacent content discipline or the required palette targets.

sources.title: Data table usage
borrowedInto: Dense evidence tables in the board keep summary-first headers and scan-friendly row grouping.
rejectedOrConstrained: Carbon operational table patterns informed board ergonomics only and did not alter the underlying product data model.
